steps.title: Identify the logical connection
steps.content: The strongest signal is Prospero's closing statement: “If thou remember'st ought ere thou camest here, / How thou camest here thou mayst.” He establishes that Miranda's ability to remember pre-arrival experiences means she should also be able to remember the arrival itself.

steps.title: Apply the connection to the choices
steps.content: Choice C directly reflects Prospero's reasoning: Miranda's capacity to recall a pre-arrival experience (the women who tended her) indicates she possesses the capacity to recall how she arrived on the island. The other choices either misidentify the emotional tone, invent psychological barriers Prospero never mentions, or contradict his confirmation that her memory is valid. That matches choice C — C is correct.
